entry.title: Re: Error in learning journey content / designing asset a...

entry.title: Preview Your App
entry.content:
Olá, estou tendo problemas para testar meu aplicativo no celular. Quando tento, ele pede um código PIN no aplicativo do celular que coloco e dá com sessão expirada.

Eu moro no Brasil.

entry.title: Re: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content: Thank you Rashid,

entry.title: How to use initial HTTP request (not from HTTP client)
entry.content:
Hi there,

I want to send a newly created HTTP request based on if_http_request or cl_http_request without using the request which is stored in the HTTP client.

I guess the default way to send a HTTP request looks kind of like this:

 

DATA(client) = cl_http_client=>create( ).
" Prepare request
client->request->set_method( ... ).
client->request->set_header_field( ... ).
...

client->send( ... ).

 

By debugging, this way is working, not least because the request object stored in the client contains a member attribute named m_c_msg that is initialized.

I would like to decouple the creation and initialization of the HTTP request by underlying the HTTP client:

 

DATA(client) = cl_http_client=>create( ).

" Prepare request
DATA(request) = NEW cl_http_request( ).
request->set_method( ... ).
request->set_header_field( ... ).
...

client->request = request.
client->send( ... ).

 

In my way the request seems not to be initialized correctly and therefore the sending fails.

Can anyone tell me if it is possible to implement my prefered solution and give me some hints?

BR

entry.title: Handle domain-driven authorization in a Node.js CAP app that uses PostgreSQL as database
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I'm developing a CAP application in Node.js (with TypeScript) that needs to check for authorizations on a domain level via associations navigation. If, for example, I have a Foo entity that has an association to Bar entity that associates itself to a Third entity that has a scalar field Field. I want to check if that Field has a specific value (maybe compared to one of the $user attributes that come from the authentication, for the sake of this example) and, if so, I would serve only the data related to a specific role that respect the given conditions.
So far so good, I can leverage the domain-driven authorizations and just navigate through the entity associations to check the value I want:

 

annotate DummyService.Foo with @(
    restrict    : [{
        grant: 'READ',
        to   : 'user',
        where: 'bar.third.field = $user.field'
    }]
);

 

But this didn't seem to work at all, I never got to have any records back. After a lot of trial and error I dug in the CAP documentation and I found out that associations navigation is not (yet, hopefully) possible outside of a SAP HANA context.

I'm using PostgreSQL so, if I didn't misunderstand that warning, I guess I have to handle it by myself in the custom handlers.

Fine, I have set up a class for the DummyService to handle the on.before event for reading Foo records. The idea is to inject the privilege check into the where clause of the req.query.SELECT object to obtain something on the line of:

 

req.query.SELECT.where = [{<query filters>}, "and", {ref: ["bar", "third", "field"]}, "=", {val: req.user.attr.field}];

 

The problem is that I can't figure out how to inject data into the where variable, since TypeScript complains about type compatibility with whatever I try to push into the conditions array.
At runtime, the where object is an Array of objects so it wouldn't be difficult at all to push something into it, but at design time stuff is a bit different. In fact, the where object adheres to the following type:

 

Partial<string & SELECT_2 & ref & val & xpr & function_call>'

 

This is what I have tried to do so far:

 

import cds from "@sap/cds";

export default class DummyService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  init() {
    this.before("READ", "Foo", (req) => {
      req.query.SELECT?.where?.push("and");
      req.query.SELECT?.where?.push({xpr: [{ref: ["bar", "third", "field"]}, "=", {val: req.user.attr.field}]});
    });

    return super.init();
  }
}

 

And the specific error is:
Argument of type '{ xpr: (string | { ref: string[]; } | { val: string; })[]; }' is not assignable to parameter of type 'Partial<string & SELECT_2 & ref & val & xpr & function_call>'.

The types returned by 'valueOf()' are incompatible between these types.
Type 'Object' is not assignable to type 'string'.

Has someone ever had to manage this kind of scenario? Is there an actual way to do this?

Thanks in advance for any help,
Federico

entry.title: Re: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content: Thank you Rashid, We know that MMBE stock is based on SLoc. But in the above MMBE pic it is showing storage bin name 'Gas WH' (from Material Master). The LS26 screenshot is shown below.

entry.title: Incorrect download link for 32 bit Crystal for visual studio
entry.content: The link for the 32-bit download leads to the 64 bit for VS 2022 and above: https://origin-az.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/file/0020000000638712024

entry.title: Re: UI5ers Buzz #55: Adaptation Project - your one stop tool for extending SAPUI5 Applications
entry.content:
Hello @hristotsolev 

We are developing a custom SAPUI5 app that we integrate with our My Inbox. The My Inbox extension that we created is an Adaptation Project, done in Business Application Studio. It was successfully deployed and our My Inbox adaptation app is able to load our custom UI5, but we are having problems with the custom approval that we trigger in the custom app using the scaffolding, since the My Inbox is not using scaffolding anymore.

My question is:

  1. Is it correct that we created an Adaptation Project for the My Inbox or should we have created an Extension Project instead? Our UI5 Version of the deployed My Inbox standard app is 1.120.11.
  2. What is the difference between the Adaptation Project and Extension Project? When should we use Adaptation Project and when should we use Extension Project?
  3. If it should be an Extension Project, how do we do this in Business Application Studio? I have only seen how to create an Adaptation Project in BAS, but not an Extension Project.
  4. If we have implemented correctly (using the Adaptation Project), is there a way to customized the approval of the requests without using scaffolding? 

Appreciate your help on this. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content:
As I said that MMBE will show report on basis storage IM. in material master you maintain bin in Plant and storage location views by using MM01. see thread for MMBE

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/storage-bin-in-mmbe/qaq-p/6664169

 

LS24 will show report on warehouse mangement bin. that is assigned in storage type. it will be different

This bin is created by LS01N in WM 

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: writing in same table without locking it
entry.content:
Here is an example how I can regenerate the error which I am getting in production:

create or replace table MyInventary (ProductName varchar(10) primary key, Quantity int);
insert into MyInventary values ('Mouse', 10), ('Keyboard', 20), ('Ram', 100), ('HDD', 15);
create or replace procedure ReceiveSinglePiecesShipping(ProductList long varchar)
begin
for ShippedProducts as mycursor cursor for select row_value as ProductInCursor from sa_split_list(ProductList) do
waitfor delay ('00:00:01');
update MyInventary set Quantity = Quantity - 1 where ProductName = trim(ProductInCursor) ;
end for;
end;

 

And then I call the procedure from two separate connections:
Connection1: call ReceiveSinglePiecesShipping('Mouse,Keyboard,RAM,Mouse');commit;
Connection2: call ReceiveSinglePiecesShipping('HDD, RAM, Mouse,Keyboard,RAM');commit;

I get a deadlock with the second connection and it is rollbacked.

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 02 - Integrated or Decentralized Warehouse Management with SAP EWM
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 02 – Integrated or Decentralized Warehouse Management with SAP EWM - What Should I Do?

You can listen to the podcast episode in English and in German on Spotify, Amazon Music, Apple Podcasts or Sway! Enjoy!

Embedded vs. decentralized EWM 

Embedded EWM is part of SAP S/4HANA and can only be used with an existing S/4 installation. It is the EWM that is fully integrated into SAP S/4HANA  and ist is used in the same system like other logistic applications MM, SD, LE etc.. There are benefits of an embedded EWM for example here is no master data distribution from the ERP module and embedded EWM supports synchronous postings. 

Decentralized EWM means a separate installation that is supplied with the necessary data via native SAP to SAP interfaces, but on the other hand can work completely autonomously.

SAP EWM Deployment Options

 

Decision Criteria 

There are functional, operational, and strategic aspects that should be considered when you have to take a decision. 

Decision criteria embedded vs. decentral EWM

1) Functional decision criteria

The first thing to consider when making this decision is the functional differences between the two options: To make an example: If you want to connect several ERP clients or ERP systems to an EWM, you cannot use an embedded EWM for this but only a decentralized EWM. Beyond that capability there are rather soft functional criteria such as synchronous production integration in embedded EWM, which of course provides real added value especially for production-related warehouses. Soft criteria however means also that the processes are  supported in embedded EWM as well as in decentralized EWM. Often the differences are rather technical - from an end-user perspective there is normally no differences. 

The functional differences are described for each release in the corresponding release notes. There is also an PDF attachment to the notes which shows the differences in transparent table format: 

Excerpt from the functional comparison for SAP EWM on S/4HANA 2023

 

SAP Note 3376455 - Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS00, FPS01 and FPS02
SAP Note 3376453 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS00, FPS01 and FPS02: Release information and restrictions for EWM in SAP S/4HANA

2) Operational decision criteria

Operational decision criteria assess the influence of a deployment on the operation of the solution. The independence of warehouse management from the ERP system for example is one of the major operational aspects. Independence can mean different things: For example the fact that if there is a downtime in the ERP, the warehouse can continue to work for the time being. Purchase orders that have already been distributed can be received and distributed outbound deliveries can be picked and shipped. The standard integration of both systems ensures that everything is posted correctly after the connection is restored.

This is ensured by the native SAP to SAP integration between EWM and ERP which uses qRFCs (queued remote function calls) for the exchange of transactional data which offer an error-tolerant integration close to real time. This native integration supports for example the distribution of purchase orders or late changes of customer orders and outbound deliveries on ERP side with automatic updates on EWM side.  

Another operational aspect is the performance and latency of the EWM systems. It can make sense or it can be necessary to operate EWM system close to the  warehouses. This does not mean that the systems have to be at the warehouse location, this is in general not necessary. But it could be required that the data center of a hypersaler is at least in the same region than the warehouse for example in Europe, Americas or in Asia. In this case a decentralized EWM is necessary if there is a single ERP system hosted for example in Europe. 

A decentralized EWM also gives more independence when it comes to maintenance and release cycles. Embedded EWM implementations must be upgraded together with the SAP S/4HANA ERP system. This can lead to bottlenecks in project and test capacities or you simply don't want to take the risk of a joint upgrade for all divisions and all warehouses.

Technical dependency can also play a role. In a decentralized EWM landscape customers do not have to coordinate with the entire SAP S/4HANA system when importing process changes. This can definitely increase the flexibility of logistics and IT departments. And finally customers have to check whether their SAP S/4HANA system has sufficient resources to operate the entire warehouse management - it's about HANA memory, application servers and so on. 

There are technical capabilities for 'embedded' EWM to get the technical infrastructure level more similar to the 'decentralized' EWM in which a whole ABAP system is dedicated for EWM for example by the placement of the EWM table group to a dedicated HANA DB scale-out node or by using dedicated application servers only for EWM users. Please check the following SAP notes for more details: 

1423066 - Optimization of the performance in EWM
2950948 - EWM table group for placement on HANA DB scale-out node

To clarify system performance aspect a greenfield system sizing should also be made as part of the deployment decision. Especially for high-volume and high performance warehouses with a lot of warehouse automation the sizing is a decisive point. The topic of automation is also always an important point: With EWM the whole material flow can be control with the integrated material flow system (EWM-MFS). External material flow systems are no longer required and EWM communicates directly with the PLCs.

Warehouse automation is a strong argument for the decentralized approach - but not one that excludes the embedded EWM option. You really have to check the specific requirements. There are SAP EWM customers who control their automated warehouses directly with embedded EWM. This can also work without any problems. A sizing you is highly recommended when automation is used. 

Sizing for SAP Extended Warehouse Management

 Please check the following links to get more information about sizing for EWM: 

SAP Sizing - HANA based Quicksizer 
SAP Note 1896197 - Sizing of Extended Warehouse Management - Best Practices

2) Strategic decision criteria

The central question when it comes to strategic decision criteria is “how will my business and my logistics develop in the future?". Answers to questions like "Do we expect strong growth", "do we have an automation strategy" or "will we have many new logistics sites" influence the decision and can rather speak for a decentralized approach - to ensure independence and performance. 

It is also conceivable to start the S/4HANA journey with a decentralized EWM on S/4HANA: We call this "logistics first approach". Customers can thus reduce the complexity of a transformation, as they only have to deal with warehouse logistics in the first step.  Later the other SAP modules follow. The integration of the decentralized EWM on S/4HANA is then only be switched from ECC to S/4HANA. Thanks to the same technical integration the complexity of switching is low and the effort is manageable.

Logistics first approach with SAP EWM

Hybrid approaches are also possible and on the roadmap of many SAP customers. This means that embedded EWM is for example used for smaller sales warehouses or production-related warehouses and decentralized EWM is used for the large distribution centers. The big advantage is that customers can rely on one single solution for their whole intralogistics - in production and distribution - since the source code and the application is the same. Even re-use of customer specific EWM extensions is in a hybrid landscape possible. 

Summary  

The answer to the question "embedded or decentralized EWM - what should I do?" depends on many aspects. In addition it is not simply possible to switch between the deployment options just by configuration. It is possible due to the fact that there is only one EWM allowing both options but the switch is always a migration project. In order to come to a good decision, it is required that customers do an individual assessment of the criteria mentioned above. Assessment tools like a utility analysis, decision trees other methods can support this process.

Helpful Links: 

SAP Note 1606493 - SAP EWM Deployment Options Best Practices

Please visit our SAP EWM Podcast Landing Page with all Episodes, Blog Posts and Podcast Notes!

entry.title: Re: Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to get your answer faster!

entry.title: Admin Proxy in the New Learning Experience
entry.content: The Admin Proxy in the New Learning Experience now allows the Admin to Start Course on behalf of the user.  Is that expected behaviour?  In the legacy version the Admin wasn't able to see "START COURSE" next to the course.  They should not be able to complete a course on behalf of the user.

entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Merge dimension using left of string

If yes, who will be in charge to configure it, partner or SAP, and what cautions we need to take care regarding license?

 

Thank you.

M.N.

entry.title: Re: OPEN ODS VIEW TO HANDLE 2 MILLING RECORDS
entry.content:
What exactly is the problem? Where are you stuck?

For start, I would suggest to go through these:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/bwplaolap/3361385376.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace -Send shipment status to customer/buyer using easy r
entry.content: You don't need E2A script to pass parameter values. The 'Placeholeder Name' and Value mapping in email rule screen does this.

entry.title: Re: can I use CORRESPONDING # for deep internal tables?
entry.content: @sh4il3sh it should work for internal tables too. Maybe create a sample test code (including local TYPES) and post it here.

entry.title: Re: HIERARCHIN IN OPEN ODS VIEW
entry.content:
ODSV only supports these semantics:

  • Facts
  • Texts
  • Master Data

See the document:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/04030263a0d041309a039fa3ea586720/e7e9e52dbf114a24bfa3a817f7ea3322.html?locale=en-US

If you need hierarchies, you need a characteristic with "Hierarchies" enabled. The hierarchies can be either local (load or create them in your BW) or it can be remote:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/ab216bfb8b2b452a860267b1e40e270f/1dec6d36681e4a0d8806a63cb925b9ad.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Best way to generate Microsoft Excel xlsx from template in ABAP
entry.content:
Hello,

Very good solution, @antonsikidin . Do you have an example of how to export an image to an excel file?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Downpayment request from sales order for US not being cleared by incoming payment
entry.content:
We have an issue with DP request for US where the tax calculation happens via taxjar integration and tax code config in SAP has 0%. The DP request created from sales order billing cannot be cleared with incoming payment with the following error message - Tax entry not possible in this item (Message no. FF724). However, the F2 invoice document can be cleared with incoming payment which means all our downpayments for US remains uncleared. This works fine for non-US company code where system is calculating the tax. 

However, if we try to create a manual FI DP request for US with some tax value and clear the same with incoming payment, this works fine. 

The issue happens only when we try to get it integrated with SD with FAZ doc type for DP req and it is not letting us go ahead in clearing the DP request with payment as in any normal std flow.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting- Anonymize Candidate Profile and Applications
entry.content: What does the candidate see in their Job Applications webpage after anonymisation.

entry.title: Simple diagnostic installation error - Connect to <hostname>: 1129 failed: NIECONN_REFUSED(-10)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Whie installing simple diagnostic agent(SDA) via agent admin UI we are getting above error message.

Please suggest me here.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to use initial HTTP request (not from HTTP client)
entry.content:
When creating the new request by using a system call like in the original SAP code I receive a 401 response.

 

SYSTEM-CALL ict "#EC CI_SYSTEMCALL
  DID
    ihttp_scid_create_message
  PARAMETERS
    1
    message_pointer
    error.

DATA(request) = NEW cl_http_request( c_msg = message_pointer ).

EDIT: Solved. Added the authentication credentials to the request by calling

request->set_authorization( .. )

Now it works. But I'm still not sure if this is a recommended solution to solve this.
Not sure about the side effects and functionality of the system call. Guess that it simply creates a new memory buffer to store the request...

entry.title: Re: Merge dimension using left of string
entry.content: You can merge variables with dimensions from BI4.2 SP3 onwards

entry.title: Re: Modify the Financial Review booklet
entry.content: Thank you very much for sharing!

entry.title: SAC exception aggregation type is lost after import job execution
entry.content:
Hi all

we have an SAC import model which is updated daily from BW query. Among other data which are loaded, we have some accounts which have exception aggregation type and exception aggregation dimension maintained and it  behaves in stories as expected.

The issue is that since Monday 18th Nov. after the import job runs and loads data to the model the aggregation type gets lost and the model has validation errors. Does anyone else has the same issue?

thank you

entry.title: Re: Anonymise Selected Job Applications in Data Retention Management
entry.content: What does the candidate see in their Job Applications webpage after application anonymisation.

entry.title: Re: Best way to generate Microsoft Excel xlsx from template in ABAP
entry.content: @joanacarolas use the original abap2xlsx and check the samples.

entry.title: Is it possible to get a list of users in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hello,

in SAP BTP I use Cloud Identity Services, which has been integrated with AD via Identity Providers. In BTP, in the Security->Users after a successful login, a user is created as a shadow user. Is it possible to use some API to get a list of shadow users? Or get a list of users directly from AD?

Thank you for any help

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja : For Business Suite on HANA(SoH) migration, will any of the project type support creation of deletion transport request to remove obsolete custom codes in ERP during SoH migration? I am using Custom code migration app in BTP.

I tried Custom code analysis project, but I dont find the option to create a deletion transport request.

If custom code migration app will not work for Suite on HANA migration, is there any plans to support in future as part of custom code analysis project type or a different project type?

Thanks for your response!!

entry.title: Re: Can I create Adaptation Project from my custom Fiori...
entry.content:
Hi @beampakorn.

With a custom ALP app what you need to do is create an extension controller or fragment manually and add your logic. You can't use Adaptation Project which is for standard SAP apps.

Take a look at this example here. 

You can right-click on the webapps folder of your app and try Guided Development options. But I didn't find one that would create an extension controller for an ALP app (there is one for List Report). So what I ended up doing was define a controller extension in my custom ALP app:

Then created a controller file manually in a new folder in webapps > ext > controller:

When I did a build it actually crated the Change file for me (you can actually create change files manually, but not recommended as far as I understand). I'm in an on-premise scenario but it should work the same way in the cloud. Let me know how it works out for you.

Ramin.

entry.title: Re: customer Credit group is not automatically moving fro...
entry.content:
Have you checked the assignment of the credit control/sales area in Customizing (OVFL)?

  • You mentioned display transactions, not change transactions?

entry.title: Re: SOIN -EXCEL data Binary conversion issue - S050 method - PROCESS_INBOUND - cont_hex[]
entry.content: Light XLSX reader: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/quick-n-dirty-solution-for-parsing-xlsx-files-on-the-go/ba-p/13463084

entry.title: S/4 HANA Product Compliance, SDS Shipment goes to "Completed: Shipment Not Required"
entry.content:
Hi All, I am using S/4 HANA Cloud Product Compliance and working on setting up SDS shipment.

When using manual SDS shipment, I am able to select an available and send to an email. In the "SDS Shipment Monitoring" app I am able to see the SDS Shipment. I notice three issues I need some help and guidance.

1. The shipment of the SDS says "Completed: Shipment Not Required". I looked at the help and it says the status refers to SDS shipments for customer who had already received the SDS. But this is the first time I triggered the SDS. Is there a way to force send the SDS? 

2. The coversheet PDF is getting generated as attachment. But the output generation is in "In Preparation" status. Is there any general job or program needs to be done to send the SDS email?

entry.title: Re: How do you want to find out about SAP BTP news and innovations? We want to know!
entry.content:
Hi @cecihuergo 

All formats are great, they are very valuable.

I prefer YouTube videos for practical reasons and especially for the language issue, when I am very interested in a content I use the subtitles that it generates automatically, that is, if I am not mistaken, the other alternatives do not offer them.

However, I repeat, I love all the formats they use and I take this opportunity to thank them for all the knowledge they share day by day, I hope that in 2025 they will do the same or better and we will continue enjoying the content.

Best regards,

Erick

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023 and Concur-Integration
entry.content:
Dear all,

we are moving from SAP ECC to S/4 HANA 2023 (brownfield conversion) and we are using Concur.

The installed Add-Ons are CTE_FND, CTE_FIN and CTE_HCM. SAP ERP-TO-CONCUR INTEGRATION Release 1.0 SP 21.

Now when preparing the S/4 HANA system for the migration, do we have to deinstall the Add-Ons or do they have an integrated deinstallation routine? I know that the Add-Ons are not required, but in the Maintenance Planner we need this information.

Best Regards,

Afzal

entry.title: Re: Start lean with RAP (Restful ABAP programming model) #1
entry.content:
Hi Ivo, 

Thank you for your blog. I only used the RAP model with a behavior projection layer so far. I was very curious to try out your “lean approach” without a projection view and all the attributes in the behavior definition.

Unfortunately, this example doesn’t work for me. Everything activates without any errors, but no CRUD operations appear in the preview app of the V4 service.

I am using a customer table instead of I_PurchaseOrder in my root view entity, but I don’t think this should be the reason.

Is there anything I could have missed?
Best Regards,
Daniel

entry.title: How is the delivery plant determined in a sales order
entry.content:
There is a requirement to know how values for the delivery plant are determined when creating a sales order.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How is the delivery plant determined in a sales order
entry.content:
When processing an item, the system automatically attempts to determine the associated delivering plant from the master data. The entry can later be changed manually.

The system uses the following search strategy. It checks, in descending order, whether a specification exists:

  1. The customer-material info record.
  2. The customer master record of the ship-to party.
  3. The specification exists in the material master record.

If nothing is found, no delivering plant is set in the sales document item.

entry.title: Re: how to display fields before alv o/p like header data
entry.content: It's called an ALV Block List (function modules REUSE_ALV_BLOCK_LIST_*, or parameter IS_LAYOUT-LIST_APPEND='X' in the classic function module REUSE_ALV_LIST_DISPLAY). You may include anything you want between with WRITE.

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing - subscribe to MQTT topics
entry.content: hi, Under broker Basic Information, certificate chain should be uploaded for client or for server. Please clarify, SAP has not provided documentation.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to have Material tab together with TM reference in MIRO?
entry.content: Enter "02" in TM_INVOICE_CLERK parameter, you will be able to see Material TAB in MIRO.

entry.title: Re: how to display fields before alv o/p like header data
entry.content: Please embed the screenshots instead of hyperlink, it's easier to see all in one place:

entry.title: Re: S 4HANA Cloud 2408 mass asset retirement
entry.content: This question relates to https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('ABAVN')/S29PCE

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to have Material tab together with TM reference in MIRO?
entry.content: The user parameter has the following alternatives: Blank The user sees the TM Reference and PO Reference tabs, as well as the G/L Accounttab that is visible according to Customizing. 01 The user only sees the TM Reference tab and, depending on Customizing, the G/L Account tab. The PO Reference tab is hidden. 02 The user does not see the TM Reference tab, but sees the PO Reference tab, as well as all tabs that are visible according to Customizing (Material Account, G/L Account, and Contract Reference).

entry.title: Re: Archive Dunning
entry.content:
Dear All,
Quite an outdated question but as I came across the same issue recently and I am an archiving expert, I want to answer it.

The reason is that in the instruction the keys for SAP_OBJECT BUS3007 had been mixed up. The SAP_OBJECT definition for BUS3007 regarding the key fields is <BUKRS><CustomerNo> instead of <CustomerNo><BUKRS>. That's why archived documents cannot be found in the attachment list and also navigation to the corresponding customer on company code level TRX FD03 does not work via e.g. OAAD (Archived documents).

entry.title: Re: Abap how to display internal table content in the right container as a HTML page.
entry.content: Please use the buttons "..." and "</>" to display your code nicely, and also to let the people test and fix your code easily. See how to do it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-to-post-code-in-sap-community-gt-2024/qaq-p/13684774

entry.title: Re: Issue with Passing FREE SELECTIONS in SUBMIT Without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN
entry.content: FREE SELECTIONS!? Is ZTEST017 really using a logical database? The selections are passed to the logical database, but what your program ZTEST017 does with this data, people can't know if you don't describe it.

entry.title: Re: How to post code in SAP Community >=2024
entry.content: it will be corrected... someday.

entry.title: Data action validation error due to data access control in SAP Analytics cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Costcenter is enabled for data access control. 

If user having all costcenters access, i didnot see any data action validation error, but if the user having the one costcenter access then i am getting validation error. Below is my data action format.

how to overcome from this issue

 

Data action

memberset costcenter =%costcenter%

If cost center = A then 

1+2+3

ENDIF

IF COSTCENTER = B THEN

4+5+6

ENDIF

IF COSTCENTER = C THEN

7+8+9

ENDELSE

entry.title: Re: S 4HANA Cloud 2408 mass asset retirement

Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Here's the What's New information about the deprecation of the app you mentioned.

The app you can use instead is called Post Asset Retirement (F7107).

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: How to get an average in a crosstab in Crystal Reports
entry.content:
Hi ,

I HAVE A CROSSTAB IN CRYSTAL REPORTS THAT HAS THE TIME PER HOUR IN ROWS AND THEN HAS THE NUMBER OF ORDERS PER HOUR AND THE NUMBER OF LINES PER ORDER PER HOUR IN THE COLUMN.HOW DO I ADD ANOTHER COLUMN TO SHOW THE AVERAGE LINES PER ORDER PER HOUR ?

Thanks

entry.title: Setup Guide for Manage Promotion process
entry.content: We are looking for setup guides on how to setup and structure the forms and workflow for the manage promotion process within Successfactors?  All guides i have found do not provide detailed steps but only test scripts for once this is setup.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2024 Release Highlights: Leveraging AI within SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting to Accelerate Hir
entry.content:
Hi @anagolderos @amphill and other experts,

Q1: From the documentation, it mentioned "The skills displayed in the Skills Compatibility section are calculated by comparing the applicant's skills, which are either validated and added manually by the applicant to their candidate profile or extracted from their resume, with the skills found in the job requisition."

From you implementation experience, how do you configure the skill section in job profile? If no skill library needed, then the skill section in job profile is not necessary to be set as "Skill" and can be long description? or the system will actually check the content of job requisition (include job profile). I'm quit confused how this operate.

Another questions are about the AI-Assisted Skills Validation for Applicants. From the documentation, it mentioned "Applicants can manage their skills by removing and adding skills from the My Skills section. "
Q2. So you know it this section will be created automatically after we enable this function or we need to do extra configuration e.g. add this section in background element? Do this section belong to candidate profile or just application or both?
Q3. Can these skill sync to Employee profile? or use in candidate search filter? I think probably not because SAP didn't mentioned about these.

entry.title: Re: Alteração no Modelo da nota fiscal não permite pesqui...

entry.title: RISE NON PRODUCTIVE TIERS - How to add weekend support
entry.content:
Dear,

Is it possible to include weekend support for S/4HANA non-productie tiers adding a SKU in the BOM?

Thank you.

M.N.

entry.title: Re: SAC - Content Usage (Open Story) per month
entry.content:
Hi there Axel,

I've had a dig through the 'System Overview' Analytic App that comes out the box.
I may be wrong here, but it seems the only way to access the underlying models is to create a copy of the app, save it as an optimised story   >> I added a new page > add a new widget then select 'existing model'

You can't find/see the models by searching for them 😞

I then used the 'Activity_Log' model as the source for a new table.

I created a table using
Dimensions:   Object Name, Date - at month granularity
I used the measure: 'Open'

The table is filtered on the dimension 'Type' = Story
                                                             Object Name  - 'A4 Example report

entry.title: Re: Do we have a certificate for datasphere? thanks ~
entry.content:
Hi alexandergregor_scivos,

I think SAP Datasphere is easier to use and more user-friendly than SAP BW/4HANA. Therefore, it is not surprising that business users can access and utilize it more than SAP BW/4HANA. I believe many users, whether they are data engineers, data analysts, or business users, are starting to use SAP Datasphere instead of SAP BW/4HANA. Therefore, there should be a variety of options available for certification in order to better meet the needs of users.

entry.title: Re: API Real Estate Contracts for Public Cloud
entry.content: Thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts.

entry.title: Registration status report with marked attendance
entry.content:
Hi all,

I used the Plateau Report Designer to add the column "Attended" to the registration status report ind LMS. Attached you can find my queries that I used. The weird thing is now, that I have classes that last 3 days. Now I want to mark the attendances of all participants so that the report shows me, which user participated on which day and which user did not for whatever reason. So in my test case I have marked the attendace of a user for days 1 and 2 but no attendance on day 3. The report shows me now the value "Y" for all days which is not correct.

Is there anyone who could help me on this?

entry.title: Re: New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know
entry.content:
Hi Alan,

thanks for your question!

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition you have the option to receive electronic invoices through the Peppol network and two additional options to receive electronic documents by email. About the two new email options:

- You can either use the combination with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, both subject for additional pricing.

- Or you can manually mass upload the received and extracted XML invoice files and validate them in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition leveraging SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, which is planned for December 2024. This solution is subject to the subscription of Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

Further details can be found in the Regulatory Change Manager.

 

If you are looking for information about testing the receiving of electronic invoices through the Peppol network, you may find the needed information on this Help Portal page:

Best Practices to Test the Integration with the Peppol Network

 

If you want to test the receiving of electronic invoices by email, you can take a test XML file (for example from here: https://github.com/itplr-kosit/xrechnung-schematron/tree/master/test/instances) and adjust it with the correct tax ID of the company code that you’re using for testing.

When you want to test the combination with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, you can attach this XML file to an email and send it to the email address that is processed in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

When you want to test the manual mass upload option, you can upload the XML file to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

I hope these answers are helpful.

Best regards,

Monika Wolf

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics Cloud: Grouping DATE values by MONTH from linked dimensions in ch
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question! It has been forwarded to the experts.

entry.title: Re: Option to trigger email for Usage Decision rejection...
entry.content:
Dear Thangaraj Thomas, 

unfortunately no out of the box integration of business workflows in our usage decision. 

However, we have business events that could potentially be consumed - this would involve development effort on the your side. 

Maybe this also helps you: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d1e58be39d884a0dbf75a7526a9acbf4/746b66d98e504e238eca043bae05ecb8.html?version=2408.500&locale=en-US

Best regards, 

Markus

entry.title: Re: Archive Dunning
entry.content: PS: This is just the solution when you use BUS3007 and BUS3008 - as LFB1, KNB1 should work as in the coding (keys changed for the new BUS-Types)

entry.title: No user token (JWT) has been provided in cds.connect.to - SAP CAP
entry.content:
Greetings everyone,

Our requirement is to connect to a destination using a JWT User Token, as shown in screenshot.

This destination works without any issue when called through a Fiori application. Our requirement is to connect to the same destination service, through a CAP application.

This is my handler. 

And this is how the destination is configured in the package.json

We keep receiving this error: "No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'OAuth2UserTokenExchange"

We have tried passing the JWT token from the request object through the headers as shown in the code, we have tried using cds object instead of transaction and we've tried even with no headers. It seems we are unable to pass the JWT token from the fiori application, to the CAP application, to the destination. Fiori to destination works perfectly. The user is authenticated on the CAP application through a destination that also uses JWT Token. We were under the assumption that this should happen automatically if we configure the destination and the package.json correctly, and so far no obvious error has come to our attention that would explain why the HTTP request is missing the JWT token, even though we're setting it in the headers. 

Can anyone help us identify the issue?

Many thanks.

entry.title: Obtain role collection for user in SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
How do I get role collection assigned to a user in sap build apps. 

Would like to know how to assign scope to a user. All I get is OpenID as the output.

entry.title: Re: Output of one table col should be passed as input to...
entry.content:
Hi Nikhil,

Thanks for your reply. This is not working in my case, i have filters on Table1, so after filtering whatever values that i get in table1 for that particular location, those values and its related information should be displayed in Table 2. Here by using linked analysis, table1 filters are not getting considered and all the values for that particular location are displayed. I think i have to use script.

entry.title: Re: How to do you move flavour changes in SAP screen pers...
entry.content:
I think it depends on setting on your SAP. I need somebody in tech team to setup so I can create a transport after a refresh of the system. But in the system where we develop flavors then I just use the SAP admin tool to select flavors and then add to a transport and then release from our DEV to quality system.

So I use the "Transport" button instead of export.

entry.title: Criteria for copying journal entries
entry.content:
Dear Community,

we have noticed that it is possible to copy some journal entries in app 'Manage Journal Entries', but not others. It is not entirely clear which requirements must be met for a posting document to be copied. We believe that we identified some criteria, for example that reverse documents can not be copied. But we still have some cases where it is not clear to us why copying the document is not possible. 

Can anyone help out with a list of criteria that needs to be met, so that a journal entry can be copied in app 'Manage Journal Entries'?

Thanks in advance!

Regards,
Niklas

entry.title: An Overview for Warehouse Order in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi All 

I am going to give you an overview for warehouse Order

Introduction- 

Document that represents an executable work package that a warehouse employee should perform at a specific time. The warehouse order consists of warehouse tasks or physical inventory items. When you move products, in other words you or Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) create warehouse tasks, then EWM groups these warehouse tasks together into warehouse orders, and makes them available for processing. When you perform a physical inventory, in other words you create physical inventory items, EWM also groups these physical inventory items together into warehouse orders. We can also use warehouse order together with wave management. We can also use warehouse orders together with wave management. Waves are used to group warehouse requests to allow the creation of multiple warehouse tasks at the same time. We can schedule the release of waves for task creation to enable efficient task management in the warehouse. These tasks can then be grouped into warehouse orders for further assignment to resources.

Configuring steps for Warehouse Orders

The configuration settings required for filters, limits, packing profiles, and the like. These settings help set up an executable work package for the warehouse resource. The resource can complete multiple open tasks in an activity area together, thus reducing travel into and out of the warehouse for each task execution.

Define Sort rules for warehouse task

Use this setting to define the sort rules and assign them to one or more sort fields. Click on New Entries, and, for each sort rule, define the fields in the warehouse tasks that can be used for sorting and whether they need to be sorted in ascending or descending order.

IMG  • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Sort Rules for Warehouse Tasks

Filters for warehouse order creation rule

Filters are used to set rules for processing a warehouse task. Embedded EWM  Warehouse Order applies filters on warehouse tasks that have not been assigned to any warehouse order to check if they can be processed by a warehouse order creation rule.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Filters for Warehouse Order Creation Rules

There are two types of filters that can be used in warehouse order creation based on the value set in Filter Type

Item filter

Item filters are used to check individual warehouse tasks for a warehouse order and are defined by setting Filter Type to I Filter at Item Level. The system checks to see if the warehouse task is within the min/max volume, weight, and pro[1]cessing limits of the item filter. It also checks whether the warehouse task has been created using a specific warehouse process type, route, wave category, and reason for movement. If a warehouse task doesn’t meet the filtering criteria, the Personal Copy for SELIM ASKRI, imaroub@wynsys.com 6 Cross-Process Settings 250 system checks to see if it can use the next warehouse order creation rule in the sequence to process the warehouse task

Subtotal filter

Embedded EWM uses subtotal filters to compare several warehouse tasks against a subtotal; they’re defined by setting Filter Type to S Filter at Subtotal Level. To use subtotal filters, it’s important to sort warehouse tasks using sort[1]ing rules with a sort field consolidation group.

Limit value for size of warehouse order

The limit value of a warehouse order creation rule specifies the size of a warehouse order. The limit value is assigned to each warehouse order creation rule. Warehouse tasks assigned to a warehouse order creation rule are assessed for limit values, which are used to set a limit on the size of the warehouse order. As soon as the warehouse task exceeds any of the limit filter criteria, it’s taken out of the existing warehouse order and assigned to a new warehouse order using the same warehouse order cre[1]ation rule. The system again applies a limit filter to the warehouse tasks assigned to the new warehouse order. A limit filter can be used to set limits for maximum/ minimum warehouse tasks in a warehouse order, maximum/minimum weight, volume, processing time, and so on

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Limit Values for the Size of a Warehouse Order

Note - You can set a limit value type (Limit Val. Type) of Physical Inventory if warehouse tasks included in the warehouse order are for physical inventory

Packing profile

 Packing profiles are used to create pick HUs during the creation of warehouse orders for outbound/picking processes. Using a packing profile, the system deter[1]mines a packaging specification and HU type using the packaging material master data for the pick HU. The system uses data such as weight, volume, capacity, and so on from the assigned warehouse order when determining the appropriate number of pick HUs in the embedded EWM system. For example, if the assigned warehouse tasks don’t fit into one HU, then the system creates another pick HU using a pre[1]defined packaging material

IMG -Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation

Note- There are multiple parameters that control the creation of pick Hus

Pack Mode

Sorting

Create HU

Assign Warehouse Task to HU

Split Warehouse Task

Split Warehouse Task Based on AUoM

SKIP Warehouse TasK

Check LWH

Warehouse order creation rule

The filters, profiles, and limits described previously are used to create a container in the form of a warehouse order creation rule that creates warehouse orders for open warehouse tasks. The warehouse order creation rule also contains the time required for the worker to complete the warehouse order

IMG- Extended Warehouse Management • Cross[1]Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Creation Rules for Warehouse Order

You can review the created warehouse order creation rules.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Overview of Creation Rules for Warehouse Orders

Determination of warehouse order creation rule

Warehouse orders are used to group multiple warehouse tasks in a single work package. To determine which warehouse order creation rule is applicable for a warehouse task, a search sequence for the warehouse order creation rule can be assigned to a combination of activity area and activity.

IMG-• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Warehouse Order • Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas

Note - You can define more than one warehouse order creation rule for an activity area and activity. This way, the system searches for a warehouse order creation rule based on the defined search sequence and picks the first warehouse order creation rule that fits the required filtering criteria. For warehouse order creation rule determination, it’s mandatory to sort the storage bins for activity area and activity.

Manual Assembly of Warehouse Orders

This function is used in the goods receipt process to delete warehouse tasks of differ[1]ent HUs from an existing warehouse order and group them in a new warehouse order using both SAP GUI and RF. This function is optimized so that you can operate it using a hand-held scanner that is installed on a keyboard filter. You can specify multiple HUs in the entry field, one after each other. EWM shows you the specified HU in the lower part of the screen, and assigns the HU to a position field, ascending. For example, you scan three HUs. EWM assigns the HU scanned first to position one, the next HU to position two, and the HU scanned last to position three. Make sure that the position of the HU in EWM matches the physical position of the HU in the resource. When you perform putaway, EWM shows you the position in the Radio Frequency dialog .

Green- you have specified a valid HU, and that warehouse tasks exist for all products in the HU. EWM has updated screen content for the total weight and total volume, and increased the number of HUs in the new warehouse order that is to be created by one.

Red-you have specified an HU, but that no warehouse task exists for at least one of the products in the HU. EWM does not update the screen content for the total weight and total volume, and does not include the HU in the new warehouse order that is to be created.

Printing Warehouse Orders

EWM allows automatic printing of warehouse orders during their cre[1]ation using smart forms or SAPscript. The warehouse order printing activity also can be carried out from the warehouse monitor. The printing is carried out via the PPF, along with the use of a condition technique. For customizing go in below path.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Warehouse Order • Print • Assign Determination Procedure.

  • EWM automatically triggers the print run from processing, or you trigger it manually
  • EWM determines the print determination procedure assigned to the warehouse process type
  • PPF calls the /SCWM/WME application with action profile /SCWM/WO and checks for the condition record for the print action definition
  • On successfully finding both the schedule and trigger condition, the system trig[1]gers the function module to print the SAP Smart Form.
  • The PPF saves the action executed and issues print logs

Some Action Definitions and Smart Forms

Note- If there are no more warehouse order creation rules left in the sequence for a ware[1]house order, the system assigns them to default rule UNDE.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: cannot create new report in LMS
entry.content: In your preview instance (you cannot do this in production), you'll be able to to download it from the report tab, if you have the Reports Developer role. We have to have SAP add the role every time we refresh our preview instance, so if it's not available in yours. you may need to open a ticket. Once you have the proper access, you should see Learning Report Designer on the Report tab of the reports page. Once you click there, it will give you the download and all the information to be able to connect to your preview instance. There is some training out there on the PRD tool and you will also need the Data Dictionary with all the tables and field if you want to add additional information to any report.

entry.title: What's the latest on Armenian localization for SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hi,

I found this community page looking for Armenian localization for SAP - but no defintive word on that here.

What's the status?

Best regards,

Dennis

entry.title: Re: cannot create new report in LMS

entry.title: Re: SAC exception aggregation type is lost after import j...
entry.content:
@thomasboub 

seems like a bug to me. may be you should open an incident if this only happened after the recent QRC upgrade.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite - Developing Custom Adapters with IDE of Your Choice
entry.content:
Same problem

java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: javax/xml/bind/JAXBException

 

<build>
<plugins>
<plugin>
<groupId>com.sap.cloud.adk</groupId>
<artifactId>com.sap.cloud.adk.build.archive</artifactId>
<version>${adk.version}</version>
<dependencies>


<dependency>
<groupId>javax.activation</groupId>
<artifactId>javax.activation-api</artifactId>
<version>1.2.0</version>
</dependency>

<!-- https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.sun.xml.messaging.saaj/saaj-impl -->

<dependency>
<groupId>com.sun.xml.messaging.saaj</groupId>
<artifactId>saaj-impl</artifactId>
<version>3.0.4</version>
</dependency>

<!-- https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.jacorb/jacorb-omgapi -->
<dependency>
<groupId>org.jacorb</groupId>
<artifactId>jacorb-omgapi</artifactId>
<version>3.7</version>
</dependency>

<dependency>
<groupId>jakarta.xml.bind</groupId>
<artifactId>jakarta.xml.bind-api</artifactId>
<version>2.3.3</version>
</dependency>

<dependency>
<groupId>com.sun.xml.bind</groupId>
<artifactId>jaxb-ri</artifactId>
<version>2.3.3</version>
<type>pom</type>
</dependency>

entry.title: Re: Issue with Passing FREE SELECTIONS in SUBMIT Without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN
entry.content: In my case, ZTEST017 is a copy of the standard program RFBLIA00 (used by F.01 transaction). This program utilizes a logical database, and there's a specific standard function module within RFBLIA00 that appears to reset my custom values for FREE SELECTIONS (e.g., PRCTR) and replaces them with default or system-determined values.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Passing FREE SELECTIONS in SUBMIT Without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN
entry.content:
What I’ve Done:

  • To address this, I copied RFBLIA00 into a custom program (ZTEST017) to gain control over this behavior.
  • I ensured that the logical database setup remains intact for the custom report.
  • My custom implementation allows me to use FREE SELECTIONS, but I’m encountering this issue only when executing the program without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN in the SUBMIT statement.

The Issue:

The clearing and replacement of my custom FREE SELECTIONS values occur due to the logic in the copied program or its associated logical database routines. This behavior happens even though the FREE SELECTIONS expressions (texpr) are populated correctly in my program.

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite - Developing Custom Adapters with IDE of Your Choice
entry.content:
The maven-bundle-plugin can be used to build osgi bundles. It would be good if sap had an example and template how to do it for the "reuse" concept. com.sap.cloud.adk.archetype-adapter-component-reuse

 

<groupId>org.apache.felix</groupId>
<artifactId>maven-bundle-plugin</artifactId>

entry.title: Re: Output of one table col should be passed as input to...
entry.content: I am not sure what do you mean when you say "all the values for that particular location are displayed". You can pass filters for dimension master data from table 1 to 2 but if by values you mean transaction data then yes you will need some scripting to achieve that.

entry.title: Re: Missing F110 Payments in BNK_MONI
entry.content:
• Did you check the log of payment run?
  • Are you actually allowed to process the two transactions (Basically you should not be allowed to process your own request, check with functional/auditors)

entry.title: Blocking Reason in LQ02
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have noticed that there is a "Blocking Reason" column in LQ02 and LX02 but it never seems to work. Is there any way for my warehouse to add this blocking reason when they move the stock from unrestricted to blocked?

 

Cheers.

entry.title: Release of SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2200
entry.content:
Discover the new DM Stack 2200: More sustainability, more flexibility! 

We are pleased to announce the release of DM Stack 2200 of our software! This latest version brings numerous improvements and new features to make your work more efficient and productive. From enhanced XBRL functionalities to new data connections – Stack 2200 has something for everyone. Read on to discover all the highlights! 

 

Create sustainability reports with SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT)

In a world where sustainability is becoming increasingly important, it is crucial for companies to transparently and understandably present their progress in this area. SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) offers a comprehensive solution for collecting, analyzing, and reporting sustainability data. With the new version of SAP Disclosure Management, you can access and process consolidated data from the SAP SCT to create meaningful sustainability reports.  

 

New XBRL extensions for ESEF and CSRS reports 

The European Single Electronic Format (ESEF) and the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Standards (CSRS) have made significant progress in recent years to improve the transparency and comparability of financial and sustainability information. A central component of these developments is the use of XBRL (eXtensible Business Reporting Language), a standardized language for representing business reports in electronic form. To further enhance the efficiency and accuracy of these reports, new XBRL extensions have been developed, which we will explore below. 

Support for Enumerations 

One of the most important innovations is enumerations. The new taxonomies can contain these, offering a predefined list of values that can be used for specific concepts in the reports. This increases the consistency and uniformity of the reports, as companies no longer need to define their own values. This reduces the risk of errors and misunderstandings and facilitates the analysis and comparison of data. More information on enumerations in our SAP Help.   

Support for Typed Dimensions 

Typed Dimensions offer a more precise and flexible way to represent multidimensional data in XBRL reports. Unlike conventional dimensions, which often have flat structures, Typed Dimensions allow the definition of complex hierarchies and relationships. This is particularly important for ESEF and CSRS reports, which often contain extensive and detailed information on financial and sustainability performance. More information on Typed Dimensions in our SAP Help. 

Single Mode for Mappings 

Another significant advancement is the Single Mode for Mappings. This allows individual concepts to be edited without changing other concepts. This granularity increases the flexibility and precision in creating reports. Companies can now make specific adjustments to ensure that their data is accurately represented and contains all relevant information. 

New Calculation and Validation Options

Additionally, new validation options have been introduced to further improve the quality and accuracy of the reports. These options allow companies to check their data against a variety of rules and standards before submission. This ensures that the reports meet all necessary requirements and contain no significant errors. More information on the new options in our SAP Help. 

Summarized Preview for a Concepts 

In ESEF and CSRS reporting, the correct representation and mapping of text concepts play a crucial role. The ability to obtain a partial preview for individual text concepts offers several advantages: 

 - Companies can ensure that each text concept is presented exactly as expected by the auditors. This reduces the risk of errors and misunderstandings. 

- The partial preview allows quick and easy verification of the correctness of the distributed mapped areas. This saves time and resources in creating and reviewing the reports. 

How does the partial preview work?

The new feature is intuitive and easy to use. When you select a text-based concept in your report, you now have the option to generate a partial preview in the right information pane. This preview shows you how the distributed mapped areas are summarized – exactly as the auditors will see them. 

 

New CSV reports for EBA

**What are the new CSV reports?** 

The EBA has introduced new reporting formats in CSV format to promote standardization and automation of data transmission. This is supported with this version through a new plugin.  

 

New feature in our Batch Client: Downloads now possible! 

Our Batch Client has received a long-awaited feature – downloads! From now on, you cannot only upload files in batches but also download them directly. More information on the new options in our SAP Help. 

Previously, you could efficiently upload files with our Batch Client, but many of you asked for a way to download the processed files as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2200
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blogs-by-sap/release-of-sap-disclosure-management-stack-2200/ba-p/13942445

entry.title: SAP Enable Now - Americas Adoption Sessions 2025
entry.content:
Please click on the links below to Register for each of the upcoming SAP Enable Now adoption sessions (Americas-friendly time zones).

Previous sessions have a link to the corresponding recordings and slide decks.

 

Date          TimeSessionRegistrationRecordings Slides
01//2025

1pm CST

 LINK  

02//2025

1pm CST LINK  
03//20251pm CST LINK  
04//20251pm CST LINK  
05//20251pm CST LINK  
06//20251pm CST LINK  
07//20251pm CST LINK  
08//20251pm CST LINK  
09//20251pm CST LINK  
10//20251pm CST LINK  
11//20251pm CST LINK  
12//20251pm CST LINK  

NOTE:  Please note that the slide presentation was created using SAP Enable Now.  The link to the slides directs you to our instance of Enable Now Manager.

The log in credentials are as follows:
User Name: anon
Password: SAPinfo2024

Logging in will allow you to access the Learner View and to see all the Books (slides) for the whole year, all in one place.
